labels.name: crypto_check_airdrops
labels.description:
Use this when you need to check if a wallet address is eligible for crypto airdrops. Scans wallet interactions against known airdrop contracts and returns eligible claims. POST variant of crypto_check_airdrops -- same params passed as JSON body instead of query string.

1. eligible: array of airdrop

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: crypto_check_airdrops · Private data: crypto_check_airdrops, crypto_check_airdrops · Public sink: crypto_check_airdrops.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: crypto_check_airdrops · Private data: crypto_check_airdrops, crypto_check_airdrops · Public sink: crypto_check_airdrops

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "crypto_check_airdrops" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "(hash e7713bce)" → after: "Use this when you need to check if a wallet address is eligible for crypto airdrops. Scans wallet interactions against known airdrop contracts and returns eligible claims. POST variant of crypto_check_airdrops -- same params passed as JSON body instead of query string.

1. eligible: array of airdrop"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
